expected_contracts: explicit-user and system-null scopes use separate bounded queries
in-memory merge preserves one global order and limit
candidate identity is deduplicated across scope lanes

feedback_files.tests/test_feedback.py:
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from trading.auto_trader import select_candidate_refs
from trading.query_store import Alert, QueryStore


def test_selector_uses_two_scope_pure_bounded_lanes_then_global_recency_order():
    now = datetime(2026, 6, 4, 1, 0)
    store = QueryStore([
        Alert(10, 7, now - timedelta(seconds=4)),
        Alert(11, 7, now - timedelta(seconds=1)),
        Alert(20, None, now),
        Alert(21, None, now - timedelta(seconds=2)),
    ])
    refs = select_candidate_refs(store, 7, limit=3, recent_first=True)
    assert [row_id for row_id, _at in refs] == [20, 11, 21]
    assert [call["scope"] for call in store.calls] == [("user", 7), ("system", None)]
    assert all(call["limit"] == 3 for call in store.calls)
    assert not any(call["scope"][0] == "or" for call in store.calls)
